title: rosa arvensis subsp. umbellata
intro: Rosa arvensis subsp. umbellata, also known as the field rose or wild rose, is a subspecies of flowering plant that belongs to the rose family. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which make it useful in treating wounds, skin irritations, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Rosa arvensis subsp. umbellata include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and promote wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and salves, which are used to treat various health conditions, including colds, fever, and skin problems.
